content.plain_english: This week in Year 5 maths, your child is working on Negative numbers. The learning focuses on: read, write, order and compare numbers to at least 1,000,000; determine the value of each digit; and read, write, order and compare numbers to at least 1,000,000. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: In lessons, children use practical resources and visual representations to explore negative numbers. They work from hands-on activities through to diagrams and then written methods, with class discussion and partner work helping every child understand and apply what they have learned.
content.home_activity: Spend 5 minutes exploring negative numbers together. Ask your child to explain what they have been learning in school — teaching someone else is one of the best ways to understand something deeply. Use everyday objects if that helps, and follow your child's lead.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to read, write, order and compare numbers to at least 1,000,000?
Was it easy or tricky to determine the value of each digit? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back over what you have learned to check and strengthen understanding.
Use your knowledge in a new or different situation.
Explain why an answer is correct using mathematical language.
Working through a challenge by choosing and using the right maths skills.

example_questions: Write 0.375 as a fraction in its simplest form.
content.times_table_tip: By Year 5 children should know all their times tables with speed and confidence. Ask quick-fire questions at dinner — mixing tables so they recall facts rather than just reciting sequences. Can they answer in under 3 seconds?
